title: Fold-Safe Local Class Posterior Features
summary: Represent each object by out-of-fold nearest-neighbor class posterior, margin, entropy, and class-distance features in standardized photometric space.
feature_strategy: Build a compact base space from raw ugriz magnitudes, redshift, alpha/delta, one-hot spectral_type and galaxy_population, plus only the standard adjacent color indices needed to define a useful distance metric. Within each training fold, standardize numeric columns on the fold-train split, fit KNN-style neighbor models using fold-train labels, and transform fold-validation rows into distance-weighted class probabilities at several neighborhood scales, top-class margin, posterior entropy, nearest-neighbor distance, mean distance to each class, and class-balanced neighbor vote fractions. For test rows, fit the same neighbor feature generator on the full training data or average fold-fitted transforms. Concatenate these local supervised-density features with the base columns before fitting simple balanced classifiers.
baseline_model_panel: Use the same small measuring panel across initial hypotheses: a balanced linear multiclass model on standardized dense features, a simple class-weighted bagged/tree model, and one compact class-weighted gradient-boosted tree model such as CatBoost with balanced class weights or LightGBM with balanced class weights.
model_panel_rationale: The linear model shows whether the engineered posterior and margin features are directly separable, while the tree and boosting models test whether local-density signals interact with raw magnitudes, redshift, and categorical fields without turning the experiment into model tuning.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold optimized for balanced accuracy. All neighbor posterior features for validation rows must be generated using only the corresponding fold-train labels and preprocessing fit on that fold-train split.
materialization_hint: Implement this as a staged feature block: first create the base photometric/categorical matrix, then generate OOF KNN posterior features for train and fold-averaged features for test, then run the baseline panel. Keep k values few and fixed, use efficient sklearn nearest-neighbor routines or class-balanced subsampling if runtime is high, and log per-class recall to detect whether QSO or STAR benefits most.
expected_signal: If useful, balanced accuracy should rise most for the linear model and per-class recall should improve near ambiguous STAR/QSO or GALAXY/QSO boundaries; posterior entropy and margin should be high-importance diagnostics for tree models and should correlate with CV mistakes.
risk: This overlaps weakly with the prior unsupervised locus-density hypothesis because both use neighborhood geometry, but the novelty is the fold-safe supervised class-posterior mechanism. Main risks are target leakage from improper OOF generation, runtime on 577k rows, and distorted distances if magnitude/color scaling or categorical one-hot weights dominate the metric.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
